title: Session card: live activity text (port board getCardSessionActivity)
prompt:
In web-ui, give the fullscreen Home Tab session card a real-time agent ACTIVITY line, like the board task card has.

Target file: web-ui/src/components/home-agent/home-session-card.tsx.

Reference: web-ui/src/components/board-card.tsx — getCardSessionActivity() (lines ~158-225) and its SESSION_ACTIVITY_COLOR map (lines ~51-59) and the single-line monospace activity row with a colored dot (lines ~797-819). This derives a short live status from the session summary: 'Thinking…', 'Read(file)', tool-call labels, 'Waiting for review', error/credit states, with a color (blue/green/gold/red/orange).

Work:
1. Extract the board's activity-derivation logic into a SHARED pure helper so both the board card and the session card use one source of truth (avoid duplicating getCardSessionActivity). Put it somewhere both can import (e.g. a shared util/derive module); board-card.tsx should be refactored to consume it with no behavior change.
2. In the session card, render this live activity line (colored dot + truncated monospace text) driven by the session summary the card already has access to. It should COMPLEMENT, not necessarily replace, the existing history message preview — decide the cleanest layout: show live activity when the session is running/awaiting-review/error, fall back to the last-message preview when idle.
3. Keep the existing status dot semantics consistent (don't regress home-session-card-derive.ts).

Add/extend unit tests for the shared helper and the card. Verify board-card tests still pass after the refactor (behavior-preserving extraction).
